dialogues: Equal groups don't just come in bags and boxes. They're everywhere. Rows of books. Stacks of plates. Groups of animals. The math works the same way.
Look at these shelves. I see rows of books. Each row is a group, just like each bag was a group.
1, 2, 3. Three rows. That's 3 groups. And in each row?
1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. Nine books. So I've got 3 rows of 9. Same pattern: groups of items.
3 times 9. And the product? Skip count by 9s: 9, 18, 27.
3 times 9 equals 27. Same equation building, different picture.
